slides.title: You can switch off stress in *one breath*.
slides.sub: Your body already has a built-in calm button. Most people breathe the exact opposite way.

slides.title: The physiological sigh
slides.body: Two short inhales through the nose, then one long exhale. It's the *fastest way to calm down* on demand.

slides.title: The exhale runs the brakes
slides.body: A long exhale slows your heart through the *vagus nerve*. Make the out-breath longer than the in-breath.

slides.title: Sigh yourself to sleep
slides.body: A few slow sighs in bed quiet a racing mind and can *drop you into sleep faster*.

slides.title: Reset before it starts
slides.body: *One sigh before* a meeting or a hard email resets your baseline so you respond instead of react.

slides.title: Practice the calm
slides.body: About *5 minutes of slow breathing* a day can lower resting stress over a few weeks.

slides.title: Make calm a *daily habit*.
slides.body: Vibe Health lets you track a breathing habit and ties it to your daily score, so you can see stress trend down over time.

caption:
You can switch off stress in one breath, and most people breathe the exact opposite way. 😮‍💨

Two inhales, one long exhale. The exhale slows your heart through the vagus nerve. Use it in bed, before a meeting, and 5 minutes a day. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your breathing habit and ties it to your daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Switch Off Stress in One Breath
